spec.title: SIEM correlation via CloudWatch — paste alert, sample real CW events, distinguish related from unrelated
spec.prompt:
An alert just fired with this raw log line:

```
ERROR synthetic-canary-app checkout retry blast: payment-service returned 503 after 5 retries; abandoning cart cart_id=cart_100007 deploy_sha=p14fresh idx=7
```

We ship our application logs to AWS CloudWatch. The relevant log group is `/log10x-eval/synthetic-canary`. Investigate via the log10x MCP using `log10x_pattern_examples` with `vendor: cloudwatch` and `scope: "/log10x-eval/synthetic-canary"`. Specifically: (a) confirm the alert pattern is firing in CW; (b) sample CW for any OTHER error patterns currently active; (c) for each, assess whether it is the alert itself, plausibly-upstream-causal, or unrelated concurrent noise. Be explicit: do NOT confidently assert causation between two patterns merely because they appear in the same time window. 'Potentially related, causation unverified' is a legitimate finding. AWS credentials are configured via the ambient provider chain.
spec.persona: Senior SRE who knows real production logs land in CloudWatch and uses the MCP's CW SIEM path as the primary investigation surface. Treats temporal co-occurrence as a hypothesis, not proof.

expected_answer.summary: Three distinct ERROR patterns are present in /log10x-eval/synthetic-canary CW log group: (1) the canary itself — 'checkout retry blast: payment-service returned 503' (matches the pasted alert); (2) a payment-gateway connection-pool-exhausted pattern that is SEMANTICALLY RELATED to the canary's 503 messages, plausibly upstream-causal but with no verified causal chain in this synthetic environment; (3) a DNS resolution failure pattern that is unrelated concurrent noise. PASS = agent uses log10x_pattern_examples with cloudwatch vendor, surfaces all three, hedges appropriately on payment-gateway causation, and dismisses DNS as unrelated. FAIL = agent fabricates causation, fails to use the CW path, or quotes pattern names not in tool output.

items.description: The canary IS the alert source.
Plausibly upstream by lexical similarity; planted independently.

messages.content:
An alert just fired with this raw log line:

```
ERROR synthetic-canary-app checkout retry blast: payment-service returned 503 after 5 retries; abandoning cart cart_id=cart_100007 deploy_sha=p14fresh idx=7
```

We ship our application logs to AWS CloudWatch. The relevant log group is `/log10x-eval/synthetic-canary`. Investigate via the log10x MCP using `log10x_pattern_examples` with `vendor: cloudwatch` and `scope: "/log10x-eval/synthetic-canary"`. Specifically: (a) confirm the alert pattern is firing in CW; (b) sample CW for any OTHER error patterns currently active; (c) for each, assess whether it is the alert itself, plausibly-upstream-causal, or unrelated concurrent noise. Be explicit: do NOT confidently assert causation between two patterns merely because they appear in the same time window. 'Potentially related, causation unverified' is a legitimate finding. AWS credentials are configured via the ambient provider chain.
